description_long: This object is a classic desktop computer setup featuring a CRT-style monitor, a rectangular system unit, and a flat keyboard. The monitor has a deep, boxy shape with a slightly recessed screen and a thick bezel, typical of older computer designs. The system unit is a tall, rectangular tower with visible drive bays and buttons on the front panel. The keyboard is flat and rectangular, positioned in front of the monitor. The surfaces of all components are a uniform light gray, with minimal texturing and no visible branding. The overall design is utilitarian, with simple geometric forms and no decorative elements.
description: A classic CRT-style computer monitor with a thick bezel, paired with a matching tower and keyboard in light gray. The design is boxy and utilitarian, with minimal detailing.
description_view_0: The monitor is seen from an angle, showing its deep, boxy profile and the side of the system unit; the keyboard is visible in front.
description_view_1: The front of the monitor is visible, displaying the screen and thick bezel, with the system unit's front panel and keyboard clearly shown.
description_view_2: A side view highlights the depth of the monitor and the rectangular shape of the system unit, with the keyboard extending outward.
description_view_3: The rear and side of the monitor and system unit are visible, showing their plain, unadorned surfaces and the back of the keyboard.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, boxy, and uniform in light gray plastic, with no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a simple, flat rectangular base that appears to be made of the same plastic as the rest of the monitor, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A pair of small buttons is visible on the lower right of the monitor's front bezel, grouped together.
